description:
# Why

The project pre-commit hook (`scripts/pre-commit`) runs `cargo test`. A git
hook always executes with `GIT_DIR`, `GIT_INDEX_FILE`, `GIT_WORK_TREE`,
`GIT_AUTHOR_*` etc. exported into its environment, and every subprocess the
hook spawns — `cargo test`, and in turn every `git` a test shells out to —
inherits them. Those vars override directory-based repo discovery: a `git`
invocation with `GIT_DIR` set ignores `current_dir(tempdir)` and operates on
the hook's repo instead.

Result: running the test suite via the pre-commit hook does not test against
tempdir fixtures — it mutates the real repo the commit is happening in.

Observed live (bl-0151, 2026-05-20). A `git commit` in a claimed task
worktree fired the hook; the hook's `cargo test` ran. `delivery_test_support::
local_repo_with_tag` does `git init` / `git add a.txt` / `git commit -qm
"seed [bl-abcd]"`, all via a `git()` helper that sets `current_dir` but does
NOT scrub `GIT_*`. With the hook's `GIT_INDEX_FILE` inherited, `git add` wrote
to the worktree's real index and `git commit` swept the already-staged `src/`
changes into a junk commit `seed [bl-abcd]` on the live `work/bl-0151` branch,
plus a stray `a.txt`. Concurrent test processes all inheriting one `GIT_DIR`
also produced `error: could not lock config file .../balls/config` panics
(`delivery_test_support.rs:26`). A plain `make check` (cargo test NOT under a
hook, no `GIT_*` in env) is unaffected — which is why this hid until now.

This is the same class as the env-var races in the parallel-test work
(bl-bfa8 HOME, bl-ad4b PATH), but the blast radius is worse: it does not just
fail a test, it writes commits onto whatever branch the developer is sitting
on.

# Scope

Make the test suite immune to an inherited GIT_* environment.

- Primary fix (one chokepoint): `scripts/pre-commit` should unset the git
  env vars before it runs `cargo test`, so ALL tests — unit and integration
  — are covered at once, with no per-helper vigilance.
- Test-helper fix: the `git` helpers that shell out without scrubbing should
  scrub `crate::git::GIT_ENV_VARS` the way the others already do. Confirmed
  culprit: `src/delivery_test_support.rs` (`git()` at ~line 20). Audit the
  rest — `git grep -l 'Command::new("git")'` over tests vs. the files that
  already loop `GIT_ENV_VARS` (bare_squash_tests, review_safety_tests,
  store_init_tests, plugin_admin_tests, plugin_policy_tests, plugin/
  consume_tests) shows the gap: delivery_remote_tests, human_gate_tests,
  state_repo_test_support, tests/common/mod.rs and several tests/*.rs do not.
  Ideally collapse the duplicated scrub-and-run git() helper into one shared
  definition rather than re-fixing N copies.

# Out of scope

The production `src/git.rs` path already scrubs (`git.rs:27`). This ball is
the test harness and the hook only.

# Origin

Root-caused while delivering bl-0151: the hook corrupted that task's own
worktree mid-delivery. Recovery was a hard reset of the work branch.
title: Test git helpers leak GIT_* env: cargo test inside the pre-commit hook corrupts the live worktree
